children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “International Nuclear Energy Act of 2025”.

children.heading: Advanced nuclear reactor
children.label: 1 Advanced nuclear reactor
children.text: The term “advanced nuclear reactor” has the meaning given the term in , except that, for purposes of this section, the reference to “reactors operating on ” in paragraph (1)(A) of that section shall be deemed to read “reactors operating in the United States on ”.section 16271(b) of this titleDecember 27, 2020December 27, 2020

children.label: A
children.text: the Government of any country that is a member of the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development;

children.label: B
children.text: the Government of the Republic of India; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Government of any country designated as an ally or partner nation by the Secretary of State for purposes of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committees on Foreign Relations, Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs, and Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an associated individual; or

children.label: B
children.text: is organized under the laws of, or otherwise subject to the jurisdiction of, a country described in paragraph (2), including a corporation that is incorporated in a country described in that paragraph.

children.heading: Associated individual
children.label: 5 Associated individual
children.text: The term “associated individual” means a foreign national who is a national of a country described in paragraph (2).

children.label: dd
children.text: any other relevant provision of law.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 8 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Energy.

children.heading: Spent nuclear fuel
children.label: 9 Spent nuclear fuel
children.text: The term “spent nuclear fuel” has the meaning given the term in .section 10101 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: is organized under the laws of, or otherwise subject to the jurisdiction of, the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: is involved in the nuclear energy industry.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established a working group, to be known as the “Nuclear Exports Working Group” (referred to in this subsection as the “working group”).

children.label: A
children.text: senior-level Federal officials, selected internally by the applicable Federal agency or organization, from any Federal agency or organization that the President determines to be appropriate; and

children.label: B
children.text: other senior-level Federal officials, selected internally by the applicable Federal agency or organization, from any other Federal agency or organization that the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: 3 Reporting
children.text: The working group shall report to the President or 1 or more Federal officials designated by the President, if applicable.

children.heading: Duties
children.label: 4 Duties
children.text: The working group shall coordinate, not less frequently than quarterly, with the Civil Nuclear Trade Advisory Committee of the Department of Commerce, the Nuclear Energy Advisory Committee of the Department of Energy, and other advisory or stakeholder groups, as necessary, to maintain an accurate and up-to-date knowledge of the standing of civil nuclear exports from the United States, including with respect to meeting the targets established as part of the 10-year civil nuclear trade strategy described in paragraph (5)(A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the working group shall establish a 10-year civil nuclear trade strategy, including biennial targets for the export of civil nuclear technologies, including light water and non-light water reactors and associated equipment and technologies, civil nuclear materials, and nuclear fuel that align with meeting international energy demand while seeking to avoid or reduce emissions and prevent the dissemination of nuclear technology, materials, and weapons to adversarial nations and terrorist groups.December 18, 2025

children.label: i
children.text: any Federal department or agency that the President determines to be appropriate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: representatives of private industry and experts in nuclear security and risk reduction, as appropriate.

children.heading: Engagement with ally or partner nations
children.label: d Engagement with ally or partner nations

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President shall launch, in accordance with applicable nuclear technology export laws (including regulations), an international initiative to modernize the civil nuclear outreach to embarking civil nuclear nations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In carrying out the initiative described in paragraph (1), the President, acting through an appropriate Federal official, and in coordination with the officials described in subparagraph (B), may, if the President determines to be appropriate, seek to establish cooperative financing relationships for the export of civil nuclear technology, components, materials, and infrastructure to embarking civil nuclear nations.

children.label: i
children.text: appropriate officials of any Federal agency that the President determines to be appropriate; and

children.label: I
children.text: ally or partner nations;

children.label: II
children.text: embarking civil nuclear nations; and

children.label: III
children.text: any other country or government that the President, in consultation with the officials described in clause (i), determines to be appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: through engagement with the International Atomic Energy Agency; or

children.label: ii
children.text: independently, if the applicable entity determines that it would be more advantageous under the circumstances to provide the applicable education and training independently;

children.label: B
children.text: assist the efforts of the International Atomic Energy Agency to expand the support provided by the International Atomic Energy Agency to embarking civil nuclear nations for nuclear safety, security, and safeguards;

children.label: C
children.text: coordinate with appropriate Federal departments and agencies on efforts to expand outreach to the private investment community and establish public-private financing relationships that enable the adoption of civil nuclear technologies by embarking civil nuclear nations, including through exports from the United States;

children.label: D
children.text: seek to better coordinate, to the maximum extent practicable, the work carried out by any Federal agency that the President determines to be appropriate; and

children.label: E
children.text: coordinate with the Export-Import Bank of the United States to improve the efficient and effective exporting of civil nuclear technologies and materials.

children.heading: Cooperative financing relationships with ally or partner nations and embarking civil nuclear nations
children.label: e Cooperative financing relationships with ally or partner nations and embarking civil nuclear nations

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President shall designate an appropriate White House official to coordinate with the officials described in subsection (d)(2)(B) to develop, as the President determines to be appropriate, financing relationships with ally or partner nations to assist in the adoption of civil nuclear technologies exported from the United States or ally or partner nations to embarking civil nuclear nations.

children.heading: Definition of United States competitiveness clause
children.label: A Definition of United States competitiveness clause
i
children.text: a cooperative agreement;

children.label: ii
children.text: a cooperative research and development agreement; and

children.label: iii
children.text: a patent waiver.

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: B Consideration
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), the relevant officials described in that paragraph shall consider the impact of United States competitiveness clauses on any financing relationships entered into or proposed to be entered into under that paragraph.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: C Waiver
children.text: The Secretary shall facilitate waivers of United States competitiveness clauses as necessary to facilitate financing relationships with ally or partner nations under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Cooperation with ally or partner nations on advanced nuclear reactor demonstration and cooperative research facilities for civil nuclear energy
children.label: f Cooperation with ally or partner nations on advanced nuclear reactor demonstration and cooperative research facilities for civil nuclear energy

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Secretary and the Secretary of Commerce, shall conduct bilateral and multilateral meetings with not fewer than 5 ally or partner nations, with the aim of enhancing nuclear energy cooperation among those ally or partner nations and the United States, for the purpose of developing collaborative relationships with respect to research, development, licensing, and deployment of advanced nuclear reactor technologies for civil nuclear energy.December 18, 2025

children.label: A
children.text: a focus on cooperation to demonstrate and deploy advanced nuclear reactors, with an emphasis on United States nuclear energy companies, during the 10-year period beginning on to provide options for addressing energy security and environmental impacts; andDecember 18, 2025

children.label: i
children.text: the demonstration and deployment of advanced nuclear reactors; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the development of cooperative research facilities.

children.heading: Financing arrangements
children.label: 3 Financing arrangements
children.text: In conducting the meetings described in paragraph (1), the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Secretary, the Secretary of Commerce, and the heads of other relevant Federal agencies and only after initial consultation with the appropriate committees of Congress, shall seek to develop financing arrangements to share the costs of the demonstration and deployment of advanced nuclear reactors and the development of cooperative research facilities with the ally or partner nations participating in those meetings.

children.heading: International civil nuclear program support
children.label: h International civil nuclear program support

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 120 days after , the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Secretary and 1 or more other Federal officials designated by the President, if applicable, shall launch an international initiative (referred to in this subsection as the “initiative”) to provide financial assistance to, and facilitate the building of technical capacities by, in accordance with this subsection, embarking civil nuclear nations for activities relating to the development of civil nuclear energy programs.December 18, 2025

children.label: i
children.text: for activities relating to the development of civil nuclear energy programs; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to facilitate the building of technical capacities for those activities.

children.label: i
children.text: not more than 1 grant of financial assistance under subparagraph (A) to any 1 embarking civil nuclear nation each fiscal year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not more than a total of 5 grants of financial assistance under subparagraph (A) to any 1 embarking civil nuclear nation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In carrying out the initiative, the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Secretary and 1 or more other Federal officials designated by the President, if applicable, is authorized to provide financial assistance to an embarking civil nuclear nation for the purpose of contracting with a United States nuclear energy company to hire 1 or more senior advisors to assist the embarking civil nuclear nation in establishing a civil nuclear program.

children.label: i
children.text: The development of financing relationships.

children.label: ii
children.text: The development of a standardized financing and project management framework for the construction of nuclear power plants.

children.label: I
children.text: light water civil nuclear technologies; and

children.label: II
children.text: non-light water civil nuclear technologies and advanced nuclear reactors.

children.label: iv
children.text: The identification of qualified organizations and service providers.

children.label: v
children.text: The identification of funds to support payment for services required to develop a civil nuclear program.

children.label: vi
children.text: Market analysis.

children.label: vii
children.text: The identification of the safety, security, safeguards, and nuclear governance required for a civil nuclear program.

children.label: viii
children.text: Risk allocation, risk management, and nuclear liability.

children.label: ix
children.text: Technical assessments of nuclear reactors and technologies.

children.label: x
children.text: The identification of actions necessary to participate in a global nuclear liability regime based on the Convention on Supplementary Compensation for Nuclear Damage, with Annex, done at Vienna (TIAS 15–415).September 12, 1997

children.label: xi
children.text: Stakeholder engagement.

children.label: xii
children.text: Management of spent nuclear fuel and nuclear waste.

children.label: xiii
children.text: Any other major activities to support the establishment of a civil nuclear program, such as the establishment of export, financing, construction, training, operations, and education requirements.

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: C Clarification
children.text: Financial assistance under this paragraph is authorized to be provided to an embarking civil nuclear nation in addition to any financial assistance provided to that embarking civil nuclear nation under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Limitation on assistance to embarking civil nuclear nations
children.label: 4 Limitation on assistance to embarking civil nuclear nations
A
children.text: to establish and submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a joint strategic plan to conduct comprehensive oversight of activities authorized under this subsection to prevent fraud, waste, and abuse; and

children.label: B
children.text: to engage in independent and effective oversight of activities authorized under this subsection through joint or individual audits, inspections, investigations, or evaluations.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 5 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: Of funds authorized to be appropriated or otherwise made available to carry out international civil nuclear energy cooperation, there is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary of State for fiscal years 2026 through 2030 up to $50,000,000 to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Biennial cabinet-level international conference on nuclear safety, security, safeguards, and sustainability
children.label: i Biennial cabinet-level international conference on nuclear safety, security, safeguards, and sustainability

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President, in coordination with international partners, as determined by the President, and industry, shall hold a biennial conference on civil nuclear safety, security, safeguards, and sustainability (referred to in this subsection as a “conference”).

children.label: i
children.text: nuclear safety, security, safeguards, and sustainability;

children.label: ii
children.text: nonproliferation and environmental safeguards; and

children.label: iii
children.text: local community engagement in areas in reasonable proximity to nuclear sites;

children.text: nuclear safety, security, safeguards, and sustainability;

children.label: bb
children.text: environmental safeguards; and

children.label: cc

children.text: cooperative financing relationships to promote competitive alternatives to Chinese and Russian financing;

children.label: III

children.label: IV
children.text: a strategy to change internal policies of multinational development banks, such as the World Bank, to support the financing of civil nuclear projects;

children.label: V
children.text: a document containing any lessons learned from countries that have partnered with the Russian Federation or the People’s Republic of China with respect to civil nuclear power, including any detrimental outcomes resulting from that partnership; and

children.label: VI
children.text: a global civil nuclear liability regime;

children.label: I
children.text: nuclear safety, security, safeguards, and sustainability;

children.label: II

children.label: iii

children.label: C
children.text: strengthen the international institutions that support nuclear safety, security, safeguards, and sustainability; and

children.label: D
children.text: foster enhanced international coordination on licensing frameworks for civil nuclear technologies.

children.label: A
children.text: the safe and secure use, storage, and transport of nuclear and radiological materials;

children.label: B
children.text: managing the evolving cyber threat to nuclear and radiological security; and

children.label: C
children.text: the role that the nuclear industry should play in nuclear and radiological safety, security, and safeguards, including with respect to the safe and secure use, storage, and transport of nuclear and radiological materials, including spent nuclear fuel and nuclear waste.

children.heading: Advanced Reactor Coordination and Resource Center
children.label: j Advanced Reactor Coordination and Resource Center
i
children.text: for embarking civil nuclear nations;

children.label: ii
children.text: to develop and assemble documents, contracts, and related items required to establish a civil nuclear program; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to develop a standardized model for the establishment of a civil nuclear program that can be used by the International Atomic Energy Agency;

children.label: i
children.text: to identify funds to support payment for services required to develop a civil nuclear program;

children.label: ii
children.text: to provide market analysis; and

children.label: D
children.text: supporting multinational standards or guidance on nuclear safety, security, and safeguards to be developed by countries with civil nuclear programs and experience;

children.label: E
children.text: developing and strengthening communications, engagement, and consensus-building;

children.label: F
children.text: carrying out any other major activities to support export, financing, education, construction, training, and education requirements relating to the establishment of a civil nuclear program;

children.label: G
children.text: developing mechanisms for how to fund and staff the Center; and

children.label: H
children.text: determining mechanisms for the selection of the location or locations of the Center.

children.heading: Objective
children.label: 2 Objective
children.text: The President shall carry out paragraph (1) with the objective of establishing the Center if the President determines that it is feasible to do so.

children.heading: Strategic Infrastructure Fund Working Group
children.label: k Strategic Infrastructure Fund Working Group

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established a working group, to be known as the “Strategic Infrastructure Fund Working Group” (referred to in this subsection as the “working group”) to provide input on the feasibility of establishing a program to support strategically important capital-intensive infrastructure projects.

children.label: A
children.text: senior-level Federal officials, selected by the head of the applicable Federal agency or organization, from any Federal agency or organization that the President determines to be appropriate;

children.label: B
children.text: other senior-level Federal officials, selected by the head of the applicable Federal agency or organization, from any other Federal agency or organization that the Secretary determines to be appropriate; and

children.label: C
children.text: any senior-level Federal official selected by the President or 1 or more Federal officials designated by the President from any Federal agency or organization.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: 3 Reporting
children.text: The working group shall report to the National Security Council.

children.label: I
children.text: civil nuclear technologies; and

children.label: II
children.text: microprocessors; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for strategic investments identified by the working group; and

children.label: i
children.text: transfer of assets to the Fund;

children.label: ii
children.text: transfer of assets from the Fund;

children.label: iii
children.text: how assets in the Fund should be invested; and

children.label: iv
children.text: governance and implementation of the Fund.

children.heading: Briefing
children.label: A Briefing
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the working group shall brief the committees described in subparagraph (C) on the status of the development of the processes necessary to implement this subsection.December 18, 2025

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the working group shall submit to the committees described in subparagraph (C) a report on the findings of the working group that includes suggested legislative text for how to establish and structure a Strategic Infrastructure Fund or an assessment of why the establishment of the Fund is not feasible.December 18, 2025

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Relations, the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation, the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources, the Committee on Environment and Public Works, the Committee on Finance, and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs, the Committee on Energy and Commerce, the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, the Committee on Ways and Means, and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Joint assessment between the United States and India on nuclear liability rules
children.label: l Joint assessment between the United States and India on nuclear liability rules
A
children.text: to assess the implementation of the Agreement for Cooperation between the Government of the United States of America and the Government of India Concerning Peaceful Uses of Nuclear Energy, signed at Washington (TIAS 08–1206);October 10, 2008

children.label: B
children.text: to discuss opportunities for the Republic of India to align domestic nuclear liability rules with international norms; and

children.label: C
children.text: to develop a strategy for the United States and the Republic of India to pursue bilateral and multilateral diplomatic engagements related to analyzing and implementing those opportunities.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , and annually thereafter for 5 years, the Secretary of State, in consultation with the heads of other relevant Federal departments and agencies, shall submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a report that describes the joint assessment developed pursuant to paragraph (1)(A).December 18, 2025

children.label: A
children.text: pursue, where in the interest of the United States, renegotiation or renewal of agreements for cooperation pursuant to section 123 of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 () (commonly referred to, and referred to in this subsection, as “section 123 agreements”) that are scheduled to expire before the date that is 10 years after ; and42 U.S.C. 2153December 18, 2025

children.label: B
children.text: pursue, in any renegotiated or new section 123 agreements, agreements that adhere to the highest standards of safety, security, and nonproliferation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary of State shall submit to the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives a report that describes United States diplomatic engagement and negotiations for section 123 agreements.December 18, 2025

children.label: i
children.text: an updated list of all countries the Secretary of State with which is pursuing or has pursued section 123 agreements during the 1-year period ending on the date of submission of the report; and

children.label: I
children.text: the participation of those countries in global nuclear fuel markets; and

children.label: II
children.text: trends in the participation of the People’s Republic of China in those markets.

children.heading: Program to enhance global competitiveness
children.label: n Program to enhance global competitiveness

children.text: expediting the conclusion of intergovernmental agreements on nuclear energy and the fuel supply chain with potential export countries;
promoting broad adherence to the Convention on Supplementary Compensation for Nuclear Damage, with Annex, done at Vienna (TIAS 15–415); andSeptember 12, 1997
encouraging favorable decisions by potential partner countries on the use of nuclear technology, fuel supplies, equipment, and services from the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: to work with allies and partners to identify alternative nuclear energy suppliers to Russia to end the reliance of those allies and partners on Rosatom;

children.label: B
children.text: to promote United States nuclear energy exports and prioritize engagement in countries where Rosatom is present;

children.label: C
children.text: to condemn the involvement of Rosatom in Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine and the role of Rosatom in endangering nuclear safety at the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant;

children.label: D
children.text: to limit the exploitation of Rosatom by the Russian Federation as a tool of malign influence;

children.label: E
children.text: to end United States reliance on the Russian nuclear energy sector; and

children.label: F
children.text: to protect national security interests by United States civil nuclear partnerships and exports and to protect the national security interests of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: to promote United States nuclear energy exports and United States nuclear energy companies, especially in nations where Rosatom is present;

children.label: B
children.text: to utilize the tools available to the United States government to discourage civil nuclear nations from partnering with Rosatom;

children.label: C
children.text: to identify what is necessary to effect a permanent decoupling of the United States from the Russian nuclear industry; and

children.label: D
children.text: to succeed Rosatom as the primary entity that can provide reactor safety and operation services to the existing Rosatom and Russian-designed and constructed nuclear reactor fleet.

children.heading: Form
children.label: 3 Form
children.text: The strategy required to be submitted under paragraph (2) shall be submitted in unclassified form, but may contain a classified annex.

children.heading: Savings provision regarding section 123 agreements
children.label: 1 Savings provision regarding section 123 agreements
children.text: Except as expressly stated in this section, nothing in this section alters or otherwise affects the interpretation or implementation of section 123 of the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 () or any other provision of law, including the requirement that agreements pursuant to that section be submitted to Congress for consideration.42 U.S.C. 2153

children.heading: Savings provision regarding authorities of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission
children.label: 2 Savings provision regarding authorities of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission
children.text: Nothing in this section affects the authorities of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: q Sunset
children.text: This section and the amendments made by this section shall cease to have effect on the date that is 20 years after .December 18, 2025
